goal: Reframe Bluepilot activation decisions so Maya/Kaya is the cross-app autonomy source of truth. Bluepilot consumes a Maya/Kaya authority decision and keeps local executor locks as guardrails; it does not own the canonical full-access grant.
scope_out: Do not call Maya/Kaya live, call providers, execute runtime, write files/GitHub/database, persist receipts, issue permits, merge, deploy, change packages, change workflows, or alter retired sandbox write routes.
claims: Bluepilot treats Maya/Kaya as the source of truth for autonomy and full-access grants.
Execution modes require a Maya/Kaya authority decision with status maya_autonomy_decision_allowed.
Full access still requires local executor and receipt-store evidence in Bluepilot.
Hard-stop categories remain blocked locally even if a malformed caller claims full access.
assumptions: Maya-core will implement the canonical authority endpoint and decision artifacts in a separate repo task.
Soulmatch and AICOS will consume the same Maya/Kaya authority pattern rather than duplicating full-access ownership.
dod: Activation decision contract names Maya/Kaya as source of truth and Bluepilot as consumer/executor guard.
Full-access preflight requires a ready Maya/Kaya authority decision.
Missing Maya/Kaya authority decision blocks execution modes.
Focused tests, typecheck, task-lock verification, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: Live Maya/Kaya calls, provider call, runtime execution, file/GitHub/database write, durable persistence, permit issue, merge, deploy, package change, workflow change, or sandbox write reopening becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Bluepilot had a local operator-mode decision surface. The user clarified that cross-app autonomy should belong to Maya/Kaya because the user works through Maya/Kaya across apps.
